title: Barclays Just Hiked Bloom Energy Price Target to $254: AI Data Center Power Story Just Got Bigger
text: Barclays increased Bloom Energy price target to $254 from $177, reflecting expanded confidence in company's AI data center power thesis. The analyst upgrade acknowledged strong Q1 2026 results and elevated full-year guidance demonstrating execution excellence. Barclays maintained Equal Weight rating, suggesting fair valuation relative to growth profile despite exceptional momentum. The upgraded price target represents 16% upside from the analyst's perspective, implying balanced risk-reward at current trading levels. Barclays commentary emphasized durability of Oracle and Brookfield partnerships as providing multi-year revenue visibility. The analyst noted that manufacturing capacity expansion to 2 GW annually supports backlog conversion and sustains triple-digit growth trajectory. The AI data center power opportunity has expanded beyond initial expectations as hyperscalers accelerate infrastructure buildout. Barclays acknowledged both upside potential from accelerating AI adoption and downside risks from potential economic slowdown or competitive threats. Multiple other analysts similarly raised price targets following Q1 earnings, with consensus suggesting fair valuation in $240-$295 range. The analyst upgrade reflects market confidence in Bloom's positioned within critical AI infrastructure ecosystem.
